wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SafeVixAI project overview section focused on the AI-powered road safety platform. Explain its purpose as a three-in-one solution addressing emergency response, legal assistance, and road reporting in India. Document the core modules including Emergency Locator, AI Chatbot, Challan Calculator (DriveLegal), and Road Reporter (RoadWatch). Describe the offline-first architecture supporting 25 major Indian cities. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's impact on road safety. Document the technology stack, deployment strategy, and open-source nature with zero infrastructure costs.

Detail service boundaries, inter-service communication patterns, and API gateway considerations. Include deployment topology across Render.com and Vercel, explaining why services are kept separate for scalability and maintenance. Document the monorepo structure and how each service maintains its own requirements.txt, environment variables, and configuration files.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SafeVixAI: AI-Powered Road Safety Platform

## Project Introduction

SafeVixAI is an innovative AI-powered road safety platform designed specifically for India's transportation ecosystem. Built as part of the IIT Madras Road Safety Hackathon 2026, this comprehensive system combines real-time emergency response capabilities with intelligent assistance for drivers and pedestrians.

### Purpose Statement
SafeVixAI addresses critical road safety challenges by providing instant access to emergency services, legal guidance, and traffic violation information through an intuitive mobile-first interface powered by artificial intelligence.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Emergency Response**: Real-time location-based emergency locator with tiered radius fallback
- **Legal Assistance**: AI-powered chatbot for traffic laws and first aid guidance
- **Traffic Management**: Intelligent routing and road infrastructure monitoring
- **Offline Capability**: Comprehensive offline functionality for areas with poor connectivity
- **Accessibility**: Multi-language support covering 10+ Indian languages

### Target Audience
- Indian drivers and pedestrians requiring immediate road safety assistance
- Traffic authorities needing efficient emergency response coordination
- Researchers studying road safety patterns and accident prevention
- Government agencies focused on transportation infrastructure improvement

## Technical Architecture

SafeVixAI follows a modern microservices architecture with three primary components working in harmony:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        FE[Next.js PWA]
        UI[React Components]
        MAP[MapLibre Maps]
        AI[WebLLM AI]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        BE[FastAPI Backend]
        CB[Chatbot Service]
        AUTH[Auth Router]
        GEO[Geocoding Service]
        ROUTE[Routing Service]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        CACHE[(Redis Cache)]
        VSTORE[(ChromaDB Vector Store)]
        OFFLINE[(Offline Data Bundle)]
    end
    
    subgraph "External APIs"
        OP[Overpass API]
        ORS[OpenRouteService]
        PH[Photon/Nominatim]
        WE[Weather API]
    end
    
    FE --> BE
    FE --> CB
    BE --> AUTH
    BE --> GEO
    BE --> ROUTE
    CB --> VSTORE
    BE --> DB
    BE --> CACHE
    CB --> CACHE
    AUTH --> OP
    ROUTE --> ORS
    GEO --> PH
    CB --> WE
    FE --> MAP
    FE --> AI
    AI --> OFFLINE
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend (Next.js PWA)**: A progressive web application built with TypeScript and React, featuring offline-first architecture with service workers and IndexedDB storage.

**Backend (FastAPI)**: RESTful API service handling emergency locator, road reporting, routing, and user management functionalities.

**Chatbot Service**: Specialized AI assistant service with RAG capabilities, supporting multiple LLM providers and multilingual conversations.

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:

- **Microservices Pattern**: Separation of concerns across distinct services
- **Repository Pattern**: Database abstraction layer for data access
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service instantiation based on configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates for emergency services and road conditions
- **Strategy Pattern**: Multiple LLM provider support with fallback mechanisms

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application**: `backend/main.py`
- FastAPI application with dependency injection
- Centralized service initialization and lifecycle management
- CORS configuration and static file serving

**Frontend Application**: `frontend/app/layout.tsx`
- Next.js root layout with theme provider and analytics integration
- Progressive web app configuration with offline capabilities
- Component composition for consistent UI experience

**Chatbot Service**: `chatbot_service/main.py`
- Standalone FastAPI service for AI assistant functionality
- Agentic RAG pipeline with context assembly and tool execution
- Rate limiting and conversation memory management

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management**: `backend/core/config.py`, `chatbot_service/config.py`
- Centralized environment variable management
- Type-safe configuration validation
- Development/production environment separation

**API Endpoints**: `backend/api/v1/__init__.py`
- Modular router organization by feature domain
- Consistent endpoint naming and documentation
- Authentication and authorization middleware integration

**Service Layer**: 
- `backend/services/roadwatch_service.py` - Road infrastructure and issue reporting
- `backend/services/emergency_locator.py` - Emergency service discovery
- `backend/services/routing_service.py` - Navigation and route optimization

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables**: Both services utilize `.env` files with comprehensive configuration:
- Database connection strings and pool settings
- External API credentials and endpoints
- Cache configuration and timeouts
- Feature flags and experimental settings

**Dependency Specifications**: 
- `backend/requirements.txt` - Core backend dependencies including FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and geospatial libraries
- `chatbot_service/requirements.txt` - AI-focused dependencies with heavy ML libraries

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**:
- FastAPI 0.136.0 for web framework
- SQLAlchemy 2.0.49 for ORM and database operations
- Redis 7.4.0 for caching and session management
- GeoPandas 1.1.3 for spatial data processing

**Frontend Dependencies**:
- Next.js 15.3.1 for PWA framework
- MapLibre GL 5.22.0 for interactive mapping
- @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.73 for offline AI capabilities
- React 19.1.0 for component library

### Integration Points

**CI/CD Pipeline**: `.github/workflows/` directory containing automated testing and deployment workflows for each service
- Backend testing with pytest
- Frontend build verification
- Chatbot service validation
- Security scanning and vulnerability assessment

**Data Integration**: 
- OpenStreetMap Overpass API for real-time infrastructure data
- OpenRouteService for navigation routing
- Weather APIs for environmental condition awareness
- Government datasets for road infrastructure transparency

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Chatbot
    participant ExternalAPI
    
    Client->>Frontend: User Action
    Frontend->>Backend: API Request
    Backend->>Backend: Service Processing
    Backend->>ExternalAPI: External Data Query
    ExternalAPI-->>Backend: Response Data
    Backend-->>Frontend: Processed Response
    Frontend-->>Client: UI Update
    
    Client->>Frontend: Chat Message
    Frontend->>Chatbot: RAG Query
    Chatbot->>Chatbot: Context Assembly
    Chatbot->>ExternalAPI: Tool Execution
    ExternalAPI-->>Chatbot: Tool Response
    Chatbot-->>Frontend: AI Response
    Frontend-->>Client: Chat Response
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

SafeVixAI provides four primary modules working together to create a comprehensive road safety ecosystem:

**Module 1: Emergency Locator**
- Real-time GPS-based emergency service discovery
- Tiered radius fallback system (500m to 50km)
- SOS sharing with WhatsApp integration
- Offline emergency map for 25 major Indian cities
- Crash detection with automatic SOS triggering

**Module 2: AI Chatbot Assistant**
- Multi-provider LLM fallback chain (Groq, Gemini, HuggingFace)
- 9-class intent detection for emergency, legal, and road assistance
- Multilingual support covering 10+ Indian languages
- Offline RAG capabilities with WebLLM
- Voice input/output with speech synthesis

**Module 3: Challan Calculator (DriveLegal)**
- Fine calculation engine using DuckDB SQL
- State-specific traffic violation enforcement
- Motor Vehicle Act legal guidance
- Global traffic law comparison database

**Module 4: Road Reporter (RoadWatch)**
- Geotagged issue reporting with photo evidence
- AI-powered pothole detection using YOLO models
- Automatic authority routing based on road type
- Community-driven road infrastructure transparency

### Implementation Highlights

**Offline Architecture**: `docs/Offline_Architecture.md`
- Comprehensive offline data bundling for 25 Indian cities
- Service worker-based caching strategy
- IndexedDB storage for user data and report queues
- Progressive enhancement for degraded connectivity

**Real-time Data Processing**: `backend/services/roadwatch_service.py`
- PostGIS spatial queries for infrastructure discovery
- Overpass API integration for live data fallback
- Redis caching for performance optimization
- Image validation and magic byte detection

**Intelligent Routing**: `backend/services/routing_service.py`
- OpenRouteService integration for turn-by-turn directions
- Multi-modal transport optimization
- Traffic-aware route calculation
- Alternative route suggestions

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> LocationDetection: GPS Permission
    LocationDetection --> EmergencySearch: Location Available
    LocationDetection --> Idle: Permission Denied
    
    EmergencySearch --> ServiceSelection: Results Found
    EmergencySearch --> LiveFallback: No Results
    LiveFallback --> ServiceSelection: External Data
    
    ServiceSelection --> SOSAction: User Selects Service
    ServiceSelection --> RoutePlanning: Navigation Required
    
    SOSAction --> WhatsAppShare: SOS Button
    WhatsAppShare --> [*]: Message Sent
    
    RoutePlanning --> Navigation: Start Journey
    Navigation --> Idle: Destination Reached
    
    Idle --> CrashDetection: Motion Sensor
    CrashDetection --> SOSAction: Crash Detected
```

The SafeVixAI platform represents a comprehensive solution for modern road safety challenges, combining cutting-edge AI technologies with practical emergency response systems. Its modular architecture ensures scalability and maintainability while providing robust offline capabilities essential for India's diverse connectivity landscape.

**Sources:**
- [backend/main.py](backend/main.py)
- [frontend/package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [chatbot_service/main.py](chatbot_service/main.py)
- [backend/core/config.py](backend/core/config.py)
- [backend/api/v1/__init__.py](backend/api/v1/__init__.py)
- [chatbot_service/config.py](chatbot_service/config.py)
- [backend/requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [chatbot_service/requirements.txt](chatbot_service/requirements.txt)
- [frontend/next.config.js](frontend/next.config.js)
- [backend/services/roadwatch_service.py](backend/services/roadwatch_service.py)
- [chatbot_service/agent/graph.py](chatbot_service/agent/graph.py)
- [frontend/app/layout.tsx](frontend/app/layout.tsx)
- [.github/workflows/backend.yml](.github/workflows/backend.yml)
- [docs/Features.md](docs/Features.md)

</blog>
